stories.title: A 200-year-old physics experiment could help build future computers
stories.desc: Scientists at Nanyang Technological University in Singapore have discovered a surprisingly simple way to create exotic light structures called optical skyrmions using a 200-year-old optical effect known as the Poisson spot. Instead of relying on expensive, highly engineered materials, they simply…

stories.title: White-beam neutron device unlocks precise control of twisted quantum waves
stories.desc: CANISIUS is the official name of the new spin-echo neutron interferometer developed at Atominstitut, TU Wien. It enables precise control of neutron waves, something that was previously impossible.

stories.title: Solving a 30-year-old puzzle about a mysterious superconducting material
stories.desc: A material made from yttrium, barium and copper oxide (better known as YBCO) has intrigued scientists since its discovery in 1987, largely because it retains its superconductive properties at a higher-than-normal temperature. However, it is extremely brittle, which makes it tricky to put to…

stories.title: Atoms tell different stories when light hits a molecule in trillionths of a second
stories.desc: Researchers have captured how a molecule redistributes energy after absorbing light, differentiating the roles of individual atoms in the process. They used X-ray flashes from the European XFEL to show that different atoms in the same molecule can reveal different aspects of the process. The study…

stories.title: New 3D thermal cloak hides objects from heat in any direction
stories.desc: Researchers have designed and built the first 3D device that can make objects invisible to heat, an advance that could transform how we protect sensitive electronics, manage heat in microchips and shield equipment from thermal detection.

stories.title: New dark matter theory could solve multiple cosmic mysteries at once
stories.desc: Dark matter may be far more complicated than scientists once believed. A new study suggests it could consist of at least two different kinds of particles that slowly separate over time, with heavier particles sinking toward the centers of galaxies and lighter ones drifting outward. This simple idea…

stories.title: Why Am I Left-Handed?
stories.desc: An invisible difference in 10% of humans poses deep mysteries in several fields at once. The post Why Am I Left-Handed? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

stories.title: 'Silly sprinklers' put in reverse to further unravel decades-old physics puzzle
stories.desc: Each summer, lawns are marked by a familiar addition: "silly sprinklers," whose loops and spirals spew water in creative ways. While seemingly frivolous in their construction, a team of mathematicians has used their design to address a long-standing mystery surrounding the laws of physics.

stories.title: The ball is round—and contrary to some keepers' views, in this World Cup it has performed just fine
stories.desc: Not every World Cup goal is a classic. Sometimes a half-hearted shot goes in as a result of little more than a goalkeeper's error. And on those occasions, goalies may be inclined to find an excuse.
